Prerequisites: Azure subscription with permissions to create Azure Container Apps, Azure Container Registry, Azure OpenAI, and monitoring resources
Azure CLI installed and already logged in in this environment (az account show must succeed)
Azure Developer CLI (azd) available in PATH
Docker daemon running and accessible from the sandbox if local image builds are required
Node.js, Python, .NET SDK, and Java toolchains available (handled by sandbox toolchains)

Steps.Description: Create a new azd environment for the azure-ai-travel-agents sample
Bind AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID in azd env to the current Azure CLI default subscription
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID $(az account show --query id -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Bind AZURE_TENANT_ID in azd env to the current Azure CLI tenant
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_TENANT_ID $(az account show --query tenantId -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Set azd environment AZURE_LOCATION to the target region
Set optional flag: API LangChain.js service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: API LlamaIndex.TS service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: API MAF Python service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: MCP Customer Query service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: MCP Destination Recommendation service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: MCP Echo Ping service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: MCP Itinerary Planning service resource exists toggle
Set optional flag: UI Angular service resource exists toggle
Set azd environment flag indicating non-GitHub Actions context
Relocate Node.js node_modules to an executable volume
Relocate Python virtual environments to an executable volume
Provision Azure resources and deploy the Azure AI Travel Agents sample (azd up)
Ensure container images are built and pushed (azd deploy – idempotent verification after azd up)
